name: PostgreSQL
description: PostgreSQL 17 relational database server. Other apps connect to it over the internal network.

inputs.label: Superuser password
inputs.help: Password for the PostgreSQL superuser. No default — you choose it. Stored encrypted.

inputs.label: Superuser name
inputs.help: Name of the superuser created on first start.

inputs.label: Initial database (optional)
inputs.help: If set, a database with this name is created on first start.

compose:
name: "slashnode-postgresql"
services:
  postgresql:
    image: "postgres:17"
    container_name: "postgresql"
    restart: "unless-stopped"
    networks:
      - slashnode
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "${secret.PASSWORD}"
      POSTGRES_USER: "${input.USER}"
      POSTGRES_DB: "${input.DATABASE}"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - "postgresql-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  postgresql-data:
    name: "slashnode-postgresql_postgresql_postgresql-data"
notes: No web UI — this is a database server reached by other apps at host "postgresql" port 5432 on the slashnode network, or on 127.0.0.1:5432 from the node (and via the app's .onion). You define the superuser password; there is no default.
